answer:
**Chapter 4 of *Kalpabuddha*** sets out a doctrine describing the attributes and subtle-body location of Lord Amitābha (one of the five meditation Buddhas in Vajrayana Buddhism).[^1]

It states that Amitābha resides in the western direction; his color is red; his consort (*devī*) is Pāṇḍaravāsinī; his seed-syllable (*mantra*) is *hrīḥ*; his type of awareness (*jñāna*) is *pratyavekṣaṇa* (introspective or reflective gnosis); his seal (*mudrā*) is *dhyāna* (meditative absorption); his implement is the *nāgapāśa* (naga noose); his pure land (*heaven*) is Sukhāvatī; he is accompanied by Lords Vajradharma, Vajratīkṣṇa, Vajraketu, and Vajrabhāsa; and within the body, his abode is the interior of the liver.[^2]

This forms a concise tantric/yogic characterization typical of Indic esoteric texts, mapping cosmic deities to psychophysical centers for visualization and meditation practices.

[^1]: *Kalpabuddha*, ch. 4 (translation), [chapter](/works/unknown-kalpabuddha-d760dc/04/translation/).

[^2]: *Kalpabuddha*, ch. 4, [p-60c6c3](/works/unknown-kalpabuddha-d760dc/04/translation/#p-60c6c3).
